codeReview.summary: Cascade delete implementation mostly matches the feature, but the persistence test suite misses a required case and includes a no-op helper call that means atomic persistence for rejected records is not actually validated for time-slot-linked data.

issues.description: The helper-created extra deduction in the persistence test is never added to the repository, so the test does not exercise the intended extra persisted record state. This weakens the claimed VAL-CASCADE-007 coverage for multi-record cascade persistence.

There is no persistence test that reloads from disk after removing a time slot with an associated rejected deduction or rejection record. The feature contract requires cascaded deletion of Facts, Deductions, and Rejections for time-slot removal plus atomic persistence on reload, but test_timeslot_cascade_persists only covers facts. A regression leaving orphaned rejections or deductions on disk after time-slot deletion would pass this suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state does not mention that Script.metadata.characters_mentioned stores character names rather than IDs, which directly affects cascade behavior for character removal and required name-based cleanup logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/models/puzzle.py:56-61 defines characters_mentioned as list[str]; src/storage/json_repository.py:187-195 removes by character name; tests/test_cascade.py:161-193 verifies name-based cleanup.

summary: Review failed. The implementation looks sound, but the added test coverage does not fully verify VAL-CASCADE-007 for time-slot cascades because persisted rejected-deduction or rejection state is never reloaded and checked after a time-slot delete.
